name: hwp-mcp
description: MCP server for reading and writing HWP/HWPX (Korean Hangul) documents — built on rhwp.
long_description: An MCP server for reading and writing Korean Hangul (HWP/HWPX) documents, built on the rhwp engine. Provides 34 tools covering text and table extraction, image extract/replace/insert, SVG/HTML page rendering, equation rendering, document metadata and Hancom field inspection, and structural editing of .hwpx files (paragraphs, tables, cells, styles, fields). Reading supports both .hwp and .hwpx; writing/editing targets .hwpx.

tools.name: read_hwp
tools.description: Read full HWP/HWPX document content as text + tables (markdown) + image listing. Args: file_path (absolute path).

tools.name: read_hwp_text
tools.description: Extract plain body text from an HWP/HWPX file (no tables, no images). Args: file_path.

tools.name: read_hwp_tables
tools.description: Extract every table from an HWP/HWPX file as GitHub-flavored markdown. Args: file_path.

tools.name: list_hwp_images
tools.description: List embedded images (mime, byte length, locator) in an HWP/HWPX file. Args: file_path.

tools.name: extract_hwp_images
tools.description: Save every embedded image to disk. Args: file_path, output_dir (optional; defaults to <file>_images/).

tools.name: replace_hwp_text
tools.description: Find and replace text in an HWPX file. v0.2: only .hwpx is supported as input/output. Args: file_path, old_text, new_text, output_path (optional).

tools.name: fill_hwp_template
tools.description: Fill multiple placeholders in an HWPX template. `replacements` is a JSON object string, e.g. {"{{name}}":"Kim","{{company}}":"Acme"}. v0.2: .hwpx only. Args: file_path, replacements, output_path (optional).

tools.name: get_hwp_info
tools.description: Get document metadata (version, page count, section count, fonts used, totals for tables/images/footnotes/equations/headers/footers). Args: file_path.

tools.name: get_hwp_page_def
tools.description: Get per-section page definition (paper size, margins, columns) and section properties. Useful for understanding document layout. Args: file_path.

tools.name: get_hwp_field_value
tools.description: Get a Hancom field's current value by name. Args: file_path, name.

tools.name: list_hwp_fields
tools.description: List Hancom-style fields (`<hp:fldBegin>`/end pairs) in the document, with name and type when available. Useful before fill_hwp_template. Args: file_path.

tools.name: list_hwp_bindata
tools.description: List ZIP entries under BinData/ inside an .hwpx (image and binary attachments). Useful before replace_hwp_image. Args: file_path.

tools.name: replace_hwp_image
tools.description: Replace an embedded image inside an .hwpx by overwriting its BinData/ ZIP entry with new file contents. `target` accepts either basename ('image1.bmp') or full entry path ('BinData/image1.bmp'). Args: file_path, target, source_path, output_path (optional).

tools.name: render_hwp_page
tools.description: Render a single page of an HWP/HWPX document as SVG. If output_path is omitted, the raw SVG string is returned inline (useful for direct LLM consumption). Args: file_path, page (0-based, default 0), output_path (optional).

tools.name: render_hwp_all_pages
tools.description: Render every page of an HWP/HWPX as SVG files in a directory. Args: file_path, output_dir (default <file>_pages/), max_pages (optional limit).

tools.name: append_hwp_paragraph
tools.description: Append a new paragraph to the end of an .hwpx document body. Clones the last paragraph's structure (paraPr/charPr/style refs) and replaces text. Args: file_path, text, output_path (optional).

tools.name: delete_hwp_paragraph
tools.description: Delete the Nth paragraph (0-based) from an .hwpx body. Args: file_path, index, output_path (optional).

tools.name: append_hwp_table_row
tools.description: Append a new row to the Nth table (0-based) in an .hwpx. `cells` is a JSON string array of cell texts (length should match table column count). Args: file_path, table_index, cells, output_path (optional).

tools.name: delete_hwp_table_row
tools.description: Delete the Mth row (0-based) from the Nth table (0-based) in an .hwpx. Args: file_path, table_index, row_index, output_path (optional).

tools.name: set_hwp_paragraph_text
tools.description: Replace the entire text of the Nth paragraph (0-based) in an .hwpx body with new text. The paragraph attributes (paraPr/style refs) are preserved; runs are collapsed into a single <hp:run> with the new text. Args: file_path, index, text, output_path (optional).

tools.name: set_hwp_cell_text
tools.description: Replace a single cell's text in a table inside an .hwpx. Args: file_path, table_index, row, col (all 0-based), text, output_path (optional).

tools.name: set_hwp_field_value
tools.description: Set a Hancom field's value by name in an .hwpx (writes the new text between the matching `<hp:fldBegin name=...>` and `<hp:fldEnd>`). Use list_hwp_fields first to discover names. Args: file_path, name, value, output_path (optional).

tools.name: append_hwp_table_column
tools.description: Append a new column to the Nth table in an .hwpx. `cells` is a JSON string array of cell texts (one per row, top-to-bottom). Args: file_path, table_index, cells, output_path (optional).

tools.name: delete_hwp_table_column
tools.description: Delete the Mth column (0-based) from the Nth table in an .hwpx (removes one <hp:tc> from every row). Args: file_path, table_index, col_index, output_path (optional).

tools.name: insert_hwp_image
tools.description: Insert a new image into an .hwpx — adds the file to BinData/, registers it in content.hpf, and appends a paragraph with an inline <hp:pic>. Args: file_path, source_path, ext (png/jpg/bmp/gif; auto from source_path if omitted), output_path (optional).

tools.name: apply_hwp_paragraph_style
tools.description: Apply paragraph formatting (alignment·indent·line_spacing) to the Nth paragraph in an .hwpx. Adds a new paraPr to header.xml and retargets the paragraph's paraPrIDRef. align: LEFT|CENTER|RIGHT|JUSTIFY|DISTRIBUTE. Args: file_path, paragraph_index, align/indent/line_spacing (any subset), output_path (optional).

tools.name: insert_hwp_table
tools.description: Insert a real OWPML table at the end of an .hwpx body (proper <hp:tbl>/<hp:tr>/<hp:tc>). headers and rows are both JSON string arrays — `headers` is a single-row array of strings, `rows` is an array of row arrays. Args: file_path, headers, rows, output_path (optional).

tools.name: merge_hwp_cells_vertical
tools.description: Merge vertical cells across rows in a single column by setting rowSpan on the first cell and removing absorbed cells in following rows. Args: file_path, table_index, col, row_start, row_count (>=2), output_path (optional).

tools.name: merge_hwp_cells_horizontal
tools.description: Merge horizontal cells in a row of a table by setting colSpan on the first cell and removing the absorbed cells. Args: file_path, table_index, row, col_start, col_count (>=2), output_path (optional).

tools.name: apply_hwp_text_style
tools.description: Apply formatting (color/bold/italic/underline/font_size) to the first run that contains target_text. Adds a new charPr to header.xml and retargets the matching <hp:run>. Color is a 6-digit hex (e.g. 'FF0000'). Args: file_path, target_text, color/bold/italic/underline/font_size (any subset), output_path (optional).

tools.name: delete_hwp_image
tools.description: Delete a BinData/ ZIP entry inside an .hwpx (effectively removes the embedded image bytes). Args: file_path, target (basename or full entry), output_path (optional).

tools.name: render_hwp_html
tools.description: Render a single page of an HWP/HWPX as HTML. Useful for AI consumption when SVG isn't ideal. Args: file_path, page (0-based, default 0), output_path (optional).

tools.name: render_hwp_equation_svg
tools.description: Render an OWPML equation script (e.g. 'TIMES LEFT ( {a} over {b} RIGHT )') to SVG. Args: script, font_size (HWP units, default 1300), color (default 0).

tools.name: create_hwpx_document
tools.description: Create a new .hwpx file from a JSON content list of {type:'text',text} items. Tables (type:'table',headers,rows) are rendered as flat text rows in v0.2. Args: output_path (must end with .hwpx), content (JSON string of items).
